# Velune — full component reference

> Complete reference for the Velune React component library (npm package `velune`): installation, theming, design tokens, and the import statement, props API, and code examples for each of the 48 components. Component documentation lives at https://velune.dev/components/<slug>.

## Installation

Setup essentials:

- Install: `npm install velune` (requires React 18+ and Tailwind CSS v4).
- Global CSS, loaded in this order — Tailwind first, then Velune tokens and utility registration:

```css
@import "tailwindcss";
@import "velune/react/theme/tokens.css";
@import "velune/react/theme/base.css";
@import "velune/react/tailwind.css";
```

- Import components from the aggregate entry point (`import { Button } from "velune/react";`) or from a dedicated subpath (`import { Button } from "velune/react/button";`).
- Optional: wrap the application in `ThemeProvider` from `velune/react` to control light, dark, system, high-contrast, reduced-motion, and brand-derived themes.

## Theming

Velune styles every component through a semantic design-token contract:

- `velune/react/theme/tokens.css` defines the token custom properties (colors, typography, spacing, radius, shadows, motion) for light, dark, and high-contrast modes.
- `velune/react/tailwind.css` registers semantic Tailwind utilities such as `bg-gs-surface`, `text-gs-text`, `text-gs-text-secondary`, `border-gs-border-default`, `rounded-gs-sm`, and `shadow-gs-1`. Prefer these utilities over hard-coded values.
- `ThemeProvider` (imported from `velune/react`) controls theme state at the application root:

```tsx
import { ThemeProvider } from "velune/react";

export function App() {
  return (
    <ThemeProvider
      theme="system" // "light" | "dark" | "system"
      brandColor="#96683f"
      mood="porcelain"
      base="porcelain"
      contrastRatio="AA"
      highContrast={false}
      reducedMotion={false}
      customTokens={{ "--radius-sm": "8px" }}
    >
      <Routes />
    </ThemeProvider>
  );
}
```

- Prefer `ThemeToggle` for light/dark switching (sun/moon icons by default). Use `useThemeToggle` for custom chrome.
- Static applications can consume the CSS tokens without a provider; `data-theme`, `data-high-contrast`, `data-reduced-motion`, and `data-brand` attributes on the document root drive the same contract.

## Design tokens

Token groups exposed as CSS custom properties and semantic utilities:

- Surfaces: `bg-gs-canvas`, `bg-gs-surface`, `bg-gs-surface-raised`, `bg-gs-surface-mist`
- Text: `text-gs-text`, `text-gs-text-secondary`, `text-gs-text-accent`, `text-gs-success`, `text-gs-warning`, `text-gs-error`
- Borders: `border-gs-border-default`, `border-gs-border-strong`, `border-gs-focus`
- Shape and elevation: `rounded-gs-xs`, `rounded-gs-sm`, `rounded-gs-lg`, `rounded-gs-full`, `shadow-gs-0`, `shadow-gs-1`, `shadow-gs-2`, `shadow-gs-3`
- Type and space: `text-gs-sm`, `font-gs-medium`, `leading-gs-normal`, `gap-gs-4`, `p-gs-4`
- Motion: `duration-gs-fast` (via `--transition-duration-gs-fast`), `ease-gs-standard`, `ease-gs-glide`

Override public semantic variables (for example `--color-primary`, `--color-surface`, `--color-text-primary`) in a scoped root instead of targeting component internals. The full contract is documented at https://velune.dev/tokens.

## Foundations

### Icon

Render accessible icons with consistent sizing and labels. Documentation: https://velune.dev/components/icon

```tsx
import { Icon } from "velune/react";
// or from the dedicated subpath:
import { Icon } from "velune/react/icon";
```

#### Props

**IconProps** (extends Omit< HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>, "children" >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| children | `ReactNode` | yes | — | Icon content — typically a Lucide (or other) SVG component. @example import { Search } from "lucide-react"; <Icon><Search /></Icon> |
| size | `IconSize` | no | md | Visual size. Default: "md". Maps to design-token spacing. |
| label | `string` | no | — | Accessible name. When set, the icon is exposed as role="img". Omit for decorative icons (they stay aria-hidden). |

Type aliases:

- `IconSize` = `"sm" | "md" | "lg"`

#### Example: Labeled icon

Use a label whenever an icon carries meaning by itself.

```tsx
import { Icon } from "velune/react";
import { Check } from "lucide-react";

export function IconLabeledExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Icon label="Completed"><Check /></Icon>
    </>
  );
}
```

### Kbd

Present keyboard shortcuts with a compact keycap treatment. Documentation: https://velune.dev/components/kbd

```tsx
import { Kbd } from "velune/react";
// or from the dedicated subpath:
import { Kbd } from "velune/react/kbd";
```

#### Props

**KbdProps** (extends HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | Keyboard key label or chord content. |
| size | `KbdSize` | no | md | Visual size. Default: "md". |

Type aliases:

- `KbdSize` = `"sm" | "md"`

#### Example: Keyboard shortcut

Show a compact command hint next to its action.

```tsx
import { Kbd } from "velune/react";

export function KbdShortcutExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <span>Open search <Kbd>Ctrl K</Kbd></span>
    </>
  );
}
```

### Text

Apply the type scale, weight, tone, and semantic element consistently. Documentation: https://velune.dev/components/text

```tsx
import { Text } from "velune/react";
// or from the dedicated subpath:
import { Text } from "velune/react/text";
```

#### Props

**TextProps** (PolymorphicProps< TElement, TextOwnProps >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| size | `TextSize` | no | — | Token-backed text size. Defaults to md. |
| weight | `TextWeight` | no | — | Defaults to regular; 4xl defaults to light. |
| tone | `TextTone` | no | — | — |
| align | `TextAlign` | no | — | Text alignment. |
| family | `TextFamily` | no | — | Font family token. Defaults to sans. |
| truncate | `boolean` | no | — | Single-line ellipsis. |
| lines | `1 \| 2 \| 3 \| 4 \| 5` | no | — | Multi-line clamp (1–5). |
| cjk | `boolean` | no | — | Applies CJK-friendly line-height and letter-spacing. |

Type aliases:

- `TextSize` = `"2xs" | "xs" | "sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl" | "2xl" | "3xl" | "4xl"`
- `TextWeight` = `"light" | "regular" | "medium"`
- `TextTone` = `"default" | "muted" | "accent" | "success" | "warning" | "error"`
- `TextAlign` = `"start" | "center" | "end" | "justify"`
- `TextFamily` = `"sans" | "serif" | "mono"`

#### Example: Type hierarchy

Express semantic hierarchy with the shared type scale.

```tsx
import { Text } from "velune/react";

export function TextHierarchyExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Text as="h2" size="2xl" weight="medium">Heading</Text>
      <Text as="p">Readable body copy.</Text>
      <Text as="p" size="sm" tone="muted">Supporting information.</Text>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Semantic tones

Communicate meaning while preserving theme contrast.

```tsx
import { Text } from "velune/react";

export function TextTonesExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Text tone="default">Default</Text>
      <Text tone="muted">Muted</Text>
      <Text tone="accent">Accent</Text>
      <Text tone="success">Success</Text>
      <Text tone="warning">Warning</Text>
      <Text tone="error">Error</Text>
    </>
  );
}
```

## Inputs

### Combobox

Filter and pick options with Empty and NoMatches states for searchable listboxes. Documentation: https://velune.dev/components/combobox

```tsx
import { Combobox } from "velune/react";
// or from the dedicated subpath:
import { Combobox } from "velune/react/combobox";
```

#### Props

**ComboboxProps** (extends Omit< HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "defaultValue" | "onChange" >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| children | `ReactNode` | yes | — | Combobox.Label, Combobox.Item, and Combobox.Empty slots. |
| value | `ComboboxValue` | no | — | Controlled selected value. |
| defaultValue | `ComboboxValue` | no | — | — |
| onValueChange | `(value: ComboboxValue) => void` | no | — | — |
| onInputChange | `(query: string) => void` | no | — | Called when the typed filter query changes. |
| placeholder | `string` | no | — | Placeholder shown while no option is selected. |
| size | `InputSize` | no | — | — |
| portal | `boolean` | no | true | Render the options panel in a portal. Default: true. |
| invalid | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| disabled | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| fullWidth | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| required | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| name | `string` | no | — | Native form field name, submitted through a hidden input. |
| form | `string` | no | — | Associates the hidden input with a form outside its DOM ancestry. |
| aria-label | `string` | no | — | — |

**ComboboxItemProps** (extends HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| value | `ComboboxValue` | yes | — | — |
| disabled | `boolean \| undefined` | no | — | — |
| textValue | `string \| undefined` | no | — | Text used for filtering when children are not plain text. |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |

Type aliases:

- `ComboboxValue` = `string`
- `ComboboxLabelProps` = `LabelHTMLAttributes<HTMLLabelElement>`
- `ComboboxDescriptionProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>`
- `ComboboxErrorMessageProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>`
- `ComboboxEmptyProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`
- `ComboboxNoMatchesProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`

#### Example: Searchable selection

Type to filter options, then pick a suggestion.

```tsx
import { Combobox } from "velune/react";

export function ComboboxBasicExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Combobox defaultValue="react" aria-label="Framework">
        <Combobox.Label>Framework</Combobox.Label>
        <Combobox.Item value="react">React</Combobox.Item>
        <Combobox.Item value="vue">Vue</Combobox.Item>
        <Combobox.Item value="svelte">Svelte</Combobox.Item>
        <Combobox.Item value="solid">Solid</Combobox.Item>
      </Combobox>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Controlled value

Own the selected key in application state.

```tsx
import { useState } from "react";
import { Combobox } from "velune/react";

export function ComboboxControlledExample() {
  const [value, setValue] = useState("berlin");

  return (
    <>
      <Combobox value={value} onValueChange={setValue} aria-label="City">
        <Combobox.Label>City</Combobox.Label>
        <Combobox.Item value="berlin">Berlin</Combobox.Item>
        <Combobox.Item value="lisbon">Lisbon</Combobox.Item>
        <Combobox.Item value="tokyo">Tokyo</Combobox.Item>
        <Combobox.NoMatches>No matching city.</Combobox.NoMatches>
      </Combobox>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Empty options

Explain when there are no options available at all.

```tsx
import { Combobox } from "velune/react";

export function ComboboxEmptyExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Combobox aria-label="City">
        <Combobox.Label>City</Combobox.Label>
        <Combobox.Empty>No cities available.</Combobox.Empty>
      </Combobox>
    </>
  );
}
```

### Slider

Select a numeric value from a range with keyboard-accessible thumbs. Documentation: https://velune.dev/components/slider

```tsx
import { Slider } from "velune/react";
// or from the dedicated subpath:
import { Slider } from "velune/react/slider";
```

#### Props

**SliderProps**

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| value | `SliderValue` | no | — | Controlled value. Pass an array for a multi-thumb range. |
| defaultValue | `SliderValue` | no | — | — |
| onValueChange | `(value: SliderValue) => void` | no | — | Fires on every value change while dragging or pressing keys. |
| onValueChangeEnd | `(value: SliderValue) => void` | no | — | Fires once interaction settles (pointer up / key release). |
| min | `number` | no | 0 | Default: 0. |
| max | `number` | no | 100 | Default: 100. |
| step | `number` | no | 1 | Default: 1. |
| disabled | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| orientation | `SliderOrientation` | no | horizontal | Default: "horizontal". |
| formatOptions | `Intl.NumberFormatOptions` | no | — | Formats the output and thumb value labels. |
| id | `string` | no | — | — |
| className | `string` | no | — | — |
| style | `CSSProperties` | no | — | — |
| aria-label | `string` | no | — | — |
| aria-labelledby | `string` | no | — | — |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | Slider.Label and Slider.Output slots. |

**SliderLabelProps** (extends HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |

**SliderOutputProps** (extends HTMLAttributes<HTMLOutputElement>)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | Custom output content. Defaults to the formatted value(s). |

Type aliases:

- `SliderOrientation` = `"horizontal" | "vertical"`
- `SliderValue` = `number | number[]`

#### Example: Single value

Label and live output pair with the slider track.

```tsx
import { Slider } from "velune/react";

export function SliderBasicExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Slider defaultValue={40}>
        <Slider.Label>Volume</Slider.Label>
        <Slider.Output />
      </Slider>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Range selection

Pass an array to render one thumb per value.

```tsx
import { Slider } from "velune/react";

export function SliderRangeExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Slider defaultValue={[20, 80]}>
        <Slider.Label>Price range</Slider.Label>
        <Slider.Output />
      </Slider>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Formatted output

Format values with Intl.NumberFormat options.

```tsx
import { Slider } from "velune/react";

export function SliderFormatExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Slider
        defaultValue={0.5}
        min={0}
        max={1}
        step={0.01}
        formatOptions={{ style: "percent" }}
      >
        <Slider.Label>Opacity</Slider.Label>
        <Slider.Output />
      </Slider>
    </>
  );
}
```

### Button

Trigger commands with variants, danger tone, loading, asChild, and buttonClasses(). Documentation: https://velune.dev/components/button

```tsx
import { Button } from "velune/react";
// or from the dedicated subpath:
import { Button } from "velune/react/button";
```

#### Props

**ButtonCommonProps**

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| variant | `ButtonVariant` | no | primary | Visual style. Default: primary. |
| tone | `ButtonTone` | no | default | Semantic color intent, combinable with any variant. Default: default. |
| size | `ButtonSize` | no | md | Control height and type scale. Default: md. |
| loading | `boolean` | no | — | Shows a spinner, sets aria-busy, and disables interaction. |
| disabled | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| fullWidth | `boolean` | no | — | Stretch to the full width of the parent. |
| type | `"button" \| "submit" \| "reset"` | no | — | — |

**ButtonProps** (| (ButtonCommonProps & Omit<ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>, "type"> &)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| as | `"button"` | no | — | — |
| asChild | `never` | no | — | — |

Type aliases:

- `ButtonVariant` = `"primary" | "secondary" | "ghost" | "text"`
- `ButtonTone` = `"default" | "danger"`
- `ButtonSize` = `"sm" | "md" | "lg"`
- `ButtonLeadingProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>`
- `ButtonTrailingProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>`

#### Example: Variants

Match action emphasis to its meaning in the workflow.

```tsx
import { Button } from "velune/react";

export function ButtonVariantsExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Button>Continue</Button>
      <Button variant="secondary">Save draft</Button>
      <Button variant="ghost">Cancel</Button>
      <Button variant="text">Learn more</Button>
      <Button tone="danger">Delete</Button>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Sizes

Use compact controls in dense surfaces and larger controls for primary flows.

```tsx
import { Button } from "velune/react";

export function ButtonSizesExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Button size="sm">Small</Button>
      <Button size="md">Medium</Button>
      <Button size="lg">Large</Button>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Composed content

Place icons explicitly before or after the command label.

```tsx
import { Button } from "velune/react";
import { ArrowRight, Check } from "lucide-react";

export function ButtonContentExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Button>
        <Button.Leading><Check size={16} /></Button.Leading>
        Confirm
      </Button>
      <Button variant="secondary">
        Continue
        <Button.Trailing><ArrowRight size={16} /></Button.Trailing>
      </Button>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: States

Keep loading and disabled behavior visible and predictable.

```tsx
import { Button } from "velune/react";

export function ButtonStatesExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Button loading>Saving</Button>
      <Button disabled>Unavailable</Button>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Render as child

Use asChild to style an existing element, such as a router link, as a button.

```tsx
import { Button } from "velune/react";

export function ButtonAsChildExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Button asChild variant="secondary">
        <a href="#docs">Read the docs</a>
      </Button>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Style recipe

Apply button appearance to a non-Button element with buttonClasses().

```tsx
import { Button, buttonClasses } from "velune/react";

export function ButtonButtonClassesExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <a href="#docs" className={buttonClasses({ variant: "secondary" })}>
        Read the docs
      </a>
    </>
  );
}
```

### Checkbox

Select independent options with group labels, descriptions, and error messages. Documentation: https://velune.dev/components/checkbox

```tsx
import { Checkbox } from "velune/react";
// or from the dedicated subpath:
import { Checkbox } from "velune/react/checkbox";
```

#### Props

**CheckboxProps** (extends Omit< InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>, "type" | "value" | "size" >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| value | `CheckboxValue` | no | — | Value used inside Checkbox.Group. |
| size | `CheckboxSize` | no | md | Visual size of the control + label. Default: md. |
| indeterminate | `boolean` | no | — | Renders the mixed state and sets the native indeterminate flag. |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |

**CheckboxGroupProps** (extends Omit< HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "defaultValue" | "onChange" >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| value | `CheckboxValue[]` | no | — | — |
| defaultValue | `CheckboxValue[]` | no | — | — |
| onValueChange | `(nextValue: CheckboxValue[]) => void` | no | — | — |
| name | `string` | no | — | Shared name applied to every child input (overridable per item). |
| form | `string` | no | — | Associates every checkbox with a form outside the group ancestry. |
| size | `CheckboxSize` | no | size to every child checkbox | Applies a default size to every child checkbox. |
| orientation | `CheckboxGroupOrientation` | no | vertical | Layout of options. Default: vertical. |
| disabled | `boolean` | no | — | Disable every checkbox in the group. |

Type aliases:

- `CheckboxValue` = `string`
- `CheckboxSize` = `"sm" | "md" | "lg"`
- `CheckboxGroupOrientation` = `"horizontal" | "vertical"`
- `CheckboxDescriptionProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>`
- `CheckboxGroupLabelProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`
- `CheckboxGroupDescriptionProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLParagraphElement>`
- `CheckboxGroupErrorMessageProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLParagraphElement>`

#### Example: Selection states

Display checked, unchecked, and indeterminate values.

```tsx
import { Checkbox } from "velune/react";

export function CheckboxStatesExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Checkbox defaultChecked>Design</Checkbox>
      <Checkbox>Engineering</Checkbox>
      <Checkbox indeterminate>Operations</Checkbox>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Checkbox group

Label the group, add supporting text, and surface a shared error.

```tsx
import { Checkbox } from "velune/react";

export function CheckboxGroupExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Checkbox.Group defaultValue={[]}>
        <Checkbox.Group.Label>Notifications</Checkbox.Group.Label>
        <Checkbox.Group.Description>
          Choose how we reach you about workspace activity.
        </Checkbox.Group.Description>
        <Checkbox value="email">Email</Checkbox>
        <Checkbox value="push">Push</Checkbox>
        <Checkbox.Group.ErrorMessage>
          Select at least one channel.
        </Checkbox.Group.ErrorMessage>
      </Checkbox.Group>
    </>
  );
}
```

### Date Picker

Choose dates with constraints, keyboard navigation, and locale formatting. Documentation: https://velune.dev/components/date-picker

```tsx
import { DatePicker } from "velune/react";
// or from the dedicated subpath:
import { DatePicker } from "velune/react/date-picker";
```

#### Props

**DatePickerProps** (extends Omit< HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "children" | "defaultValue" | "onChange" | "value" >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |
| value | `Date \| string \| null` | no | — | — |
| defaultValue | `Date \| string \| null` | no | — | — |
| onValueChange | `(value: Date \| null) => void` | no | — | — |
| min | `Date \| string \| null` | no | — | — |
| max | `Date \| string \| null` | no | — | — |
| disabled | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| invalid | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| required | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| readOnly | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| placeholder | `string` | no | — | — |
| todayLabel | `string` | no | Today | Label for the calendar's today command. Default: Today. |
| previousMonthLabel | `string` | no | — | Accessible label for previous-month navigation. |
| nextMonthLabel | `string` | no | — | Accessible label for next-month navigation. |
| clearLabel | `string` | no | — | Accessible label for clearing the current value. |
| size | `DatePickerSize` | no | — | — |
| fullWidth | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| clearable | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| locale | `string` | no | runtime locale | BCP 47 locale for labels/formatting. Default: runtime locale. |
| formatOptions | `Intl.DateTimeFormatOptions` | no | — | — |
| weekStartsOn | `WeekStart` | no | 0 | 0 Sunday, 1 Monday. Default: 0. |
| dir | `DatePickerDirection` | no | ltr | Reading direction for the field and calendar navigation. Default: ltr. |
| open | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| defaultOpen | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| onOpenChange | `(open: boolean) => void` | no | — | — |
| name | `string` | no | — | — |
| form | `string` | no | — | Associates the date value with a form outside its DOM ancestry. |
| id | `string` | no | — | — |

Type aliases:

- `DatePickerSize` = `InputSize`
- `DatePickerWeekStartsOn` = `WeekStart`
- `DatePickerDirection` = `"ltr" | "rtl"`
- `DatePickerLabelProps` = `LabelHTMLAttributes<HTMLLabelElement>`
- `DatePickerDescriptionProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`
- `DatePickerErrorMessageProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`

#### Example: Basic date picker

Choose a date with keyboard navigation and locale formatting.

```tsx
import { DatePicker } from "velune/react";

export function DatePickerBasicExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <DatePicker defaultValue="2026-07-13">
        <DatePicker.Label>Launch date</DatePicker.Label>
      </DatePicker>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Date constraints

Limit selection to an allowed scheduling window.

```tsx
import { DatePicker } from "velune/react";

export function DatePickerConstraintsExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <DatePicker min="2026-07-01" max="2026-07-31">
        <DatePicker.Label>Review date</DatePicker.Label>
      </DatePicker>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Field states

Present required, disabled, and invalid date fields consistently.

```tsx
import { DatePicker } from "velune/react";

export function DatePickerStatesExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <DatePicker required>
        <DatePicker.Label>Required date</DatePicker.Label>
      </DatePicker>
      <DatePicker disabled>
        <DatePicker.Label>Unavailable</DatePicker.Label>
      </DatePicker>
    </>
  );
}
```

### Date Range Picker

Choose a start and end date with editable segments and a shared range calendar. Documentation: https://velune.dev/components/date-range-picker

```tsx
import { DateRangePicker } from "velune/react";
// or from the dedicated subpath:
import { DateRangePicker } from "velune/react/date-range-picker";
```

#### Props

**DateRangeValue**

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| start | `Date \| null` | yes | — | — |
| end | `Date \| null` | yes | — | — |

**DateRangeInput**

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| start | `Date \| string \| null` | no | — | — |
| end | `Date \| string \| null` | no | — | — |

**DateRangePickerProps** (extends Omit< HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "children" | "defaultValue" | "onChange" >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |
| value | `DateRangeInput` | no | — | — |
| defaultValue | `DateRangeInput` | no | — | — |
| onValueChange | `(value: DateRangeValue) => void` | no | — | — |
| min | `Date \| string \| null` | no | — | — |
| max | `Date \| string \| null` | no | — | — |
| disabled | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| invalid | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| required | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| readOnly | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| startLabel | `string` | no | Start date | Accessible name for the start-date field. Default: Start date. |
| endLabel | `string` | no | End date | Accessible name for the end-date field. Default: End date. |
| clearLabel | `string` | no | Clear date range | Accessible label for clearing either date. Default: Clear date range. |
| openCalendarLabel | `string` | no | Open calendar | Accessible label for opening the calendar. Default: Open calendar. |
| previousMonthLabel | `string` | no | Previous month | Accessible label for the previous-month command. Default: Previous month. |
| nextMonthLabel | `string` | no | Next month | Accessible label for the next-month command. Default: Next month. |
| size | `DatePickerSize` | no | — | — |
| fullWidth | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| clearable | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| locale | `string` | no | — | — |
| weekStartsOn | `DatePickerWeekStartsOn` | no | — | — |
| dir | `DatePickerDirection` | no | — | — |
| open | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| defaultOpen | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| onOpenChange | `(open: boolean) => void` | no | — | — |
| startName | `string` | no | — | — |
| endName | `string` | no | — | — |
| form | `string` | no | — | Associates both date values with a form outside their DOM ancestry. |
| id | `string` | no | — | — |

Type aliases:

- `DateRangePickerLabelProps` = `LabelHTMLAttributes<HTMLLabelElement>`
- `DateRangePickerDescriptionProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`
- `DateRangePickerErrorMessageProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`

#### Example: Basic date range

Edit either date directly or choose a continuous range from the shared calendar.

```tsx
import { DateRangePicker } from "velune/react";

export function DateRangePickerBasicExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <DateRangePicker
        defaultValue={{
          start: "2026-07-13",
          end: "2026-07-18",
        }}
        clearable
      >
        <DateRangePicker.Label>Travel dates</DateRangePicker.Label>
        <DateRangePicker.Description>
          Select the first and last day of your trip.
        </DateRangePicker.Description>
      </DateRangePicker>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Booking window

Restrict both dates to a valid window while preserving range ordering.

```tsx
import { DateRangePicker } from "velune/react";

export function DateRangePickerConstraintsExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <DateRangePicker
        min="2026-07-10"
        max="2026-07-25"
        defaultValue={{
          start: "2026-07-12",
          end: "2026-07-18",
        }}
      >
        <DateRangePicker.Label>Reservation</DateRangePicker.Label>
      </DateRangePicker>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Field states

Communicate required, invalid, read-only, and disabled ranges consistently.

```tsx
import { DateRangePicker } from "velune/react";

export function DateRangePickerStatesExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <DateRangePicker required invalid>
        <DateRangePicker.Label>Billing period</DateRangePicker.Label>
        <DateRangePicker.ErrorMessage>
          Choose a complete billing period.
        </DateRangePicker.ErrorMessage>
      </DateRangePicker>
      <DateRangePicker
        defaultValue={{
          start: "2026-07-13",
          end: "2026-07-18",
        }}
        readOnly
      >
        <DateRangePicker.Label>Approved period</DateRangePicker.Label>
      </DateRangePicker>
    </>
  );
}
```

### Dropdown

Present keyboard-accessible actions, grouped commands, and selectable options. Documentation: https://velune.dev/components/dropdown

```tsx
import { Dropdown } from "velune/react";
// or from the dedicated subpath:
import { Dropdown } from "velune/react/dropdown";
```

#### Props

**DropdownProps** (extends Omit< HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "children" | "onChange" >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| children | `ReactNode` | yes | — | — |
| open | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| defaultOpen | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| onOpenChange | `(open: boolean) => void` | no | — | — |
| placement | `PopoverPlacement` | no | — | — |
| offset | `number` | no | — | — |
| disabled | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| portal | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| fullWidth | `boolean` | no | false (matches form controls) | Stretch the trigger and menu to their parent width. Default: false (matches form controls). |

**DropdownTriggerProps**

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| children | `ReactElement` | yes | — | — |
| className | `string` | no | — | — |
| style | `React.CSSProperties` | no | — | — |

**DropdownMenuProps** (extends Omit< HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "children" | "onSelect" >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |
| aria-label | `string` | no | — | Accessible menu name when the trigger does not provide one. |
| selectionMode | `DropdownSelectionMode` | no | — | — |
| selectedKeys | `Iterable<DropdownKey> \| "all"` | no | — | — |
| defaultSelectedKeys | `Iterable<DropdownKey> \| "all"` | no | — | — |
| onSelectionChange | `(keys: DropdownSelection) => void` | no | — | — |
| onAction | `(key: DropdownKey) => void` | no | — | — |
| closeOnSelect | `boolean` | no | false for multiple selection, true otherwise | Default: false for multiple selection, true otherwise. |
| className | `string` | no | — | — |
| style | `React.CSSProperties` | no | — | — |

**DropdownItemProps** (extends Omit< HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>, "children" | "id" | "className" | "onSelect" >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| value | `DropdownKey` | yes | — | Selection key for the item. Matches the value naming used by Select. |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |
| textValue | `string` | no | — | — |
| disabled | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| tone | `"default" \| "danger"` | no | — | — |
| href | `string` | no | — | — |
| target | `string` | no | — | — |
| onAction | `() => void` | no | — | — |
| closeOnSelect | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| className | `string` | no | — | — |

**DropdownSectionProps** (extends Omit< HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "children" | "className" >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |
| className | `string` | no | — | — |

Type aliases:

- `DropdownKey` = `string | number`
- `DropdownSelection` = `"all" | Set<DropdownKey>`
- `DropdownSelectionMode` = `"none" | "single" | "multiple"`
- `DropdownSelectedKeys` = `DropdownSelection`
- `DropdownSectionTitleProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>`
- `DropdownSeparatorProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`
- `DropdownItemLeadingProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>`
- `DropdownItemDescriptionProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>`
- `DropdownItemTrailingProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>`

#### Example: Action menu

Open a compact command menu from any Velune button or custom trigger.

```tsx
import { Dropdown, Button } from "velune/react";

export function DropdownBasicExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Dropdown>
        <Dropdown.Trigger>
          <Button variant="secondary">Project actions</Button>
        </Dropdown.Trigger>
        <Dropdown.Menu aria-label="Project actions">
          <Dropdown.Item value="edit">Edit project</Dropdown.Item>
          <Dropdown.Item value="duplicate">Duplicate</Dropdown.Item>
          <Dropdown.Separator />
          <Dropdown.Item value="delete" tone="danger">Delete project</Dropdown.Item>
        </Dropdown.Menu>
      </Dropdown>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Sections and metadata

Group related commands and add supporting descriptions or shortcuts.

```tsx
import { Dropdown, Button } from "velune/react";

export function DropdownSectionsExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Dropdown>
        <Dropdown.Trigger>
          <Button variant="secondary">Account</Button>
        </Dropdown.Trigger>
        <Dropdown.Menu aria-label="Account menu">
          <Dropdown.Section>
            <Dropdown.SectionTitle>Workspace</Dropdown.SectionTitle>
            <Dropdown.Item value="profile">
              Profile
              <Dropdown.Item.Description>Manage your public identity</Dropdown.Item.Description>
              <Dropdown.Item.Trailing>⌘P</Dropdown.Item.Trailing>
            </Dropdown.Item>
          </Dropdown.Section>
          <Dropdown.Separator />
          <Dropdown.Item value="sign-out">Sign out</Dropdown.Item>
        </Dropdown.Menu>
      </Dropdown>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Multiple selection

Keep the menu open while people toggle several independent options.

```tsx
import { Dropdown, Button } from "velune/react";

export function DropdownSelectionExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Dropdown>
        <Dropdown.Trigger>
          <Button variant="secondary">Visible columns</Button>
        </Dropdown.Trigger>
        <Dropdown.Menu
          aria-label="Visible columns"
          selectionMode="multiple"
          defaultSelectedKeys={["name", "status"]}
        >
          <Dropdown.Item value="name">Name</Dropdown.Item>
          <Dropdown.Item value="owner">Owner</Dropdown.Item>
          <Dropdown.Item value="status">Status</Dropdown.Item>
          <Dropdown.Item value="updated">Last updated</Dropdown.Item>
        </Dropdown.Menu>
      </Dropdown>
    </>
  );
}
```

### Form

Coordinate field values with rules or Standard Schema validation and submission state. Documentation: https://velune.dev/components/form

```tsx
import { Form } from "velune/react";
// or from the dedicated subpath:
import { Form } from "velune/react/form";
```

#### Props

**RuleObject**

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| required | `boolean \| string` | no | — | — |
| min | `number` | no | — | — |
| max | `number` | no | — | — |
| minLength | `number` | no | — | — |
| maxLength | `number` | no | — | — |
| pattern | `RegExp` | no | — | — |
| message | `string` | no | — | — |
| validator | `( value: unknown, values: FormValues, ) => void \| string \| Promise<void \| string>` | no | — | — |

**FieldMeta**

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| error | `string \| undefined` | no | — | — |
| touched | `boolean` | yes | — | — |
| validating | `boolean` | yes | — | — |

**FormProps** (extends Omit< FormHTMLAttributes<HTMLFormElement>, "onSubmit" | "onChange" >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| initialValues | `FormValues` | no | — | — |
| values | `FormValues` | no | — | — |
| schema | `FormSchema` | no | — | Standard Schema (zod 3.24+, valibot, arktype, …) validated against the whole values object. Issue paths map to Form.Item names as dot paths. Explicit field rules take precedence for the same field. |
| onValuesChange | `(changed: FormValues, all: FormValues) => void` | no | — | — |
| onSubmit | `(values: FormValues) => void \| Promise<void>` | no | — | With schema, receives the parsed (possibly transformed) output. |
| onSubmitFailed | `(errors: FormErrors, values: FormValues) => void` | no | — | — |
| validateOnChange | `boolean` | no | true | Validate on change after first blur. Default: true. |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |

**FormItemProps** (extends Omit< HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "children" >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| name | `string` | yes | — | — |
| rules | `FormRule[]` | no | — | — |
| required | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| noStyle | `boolean` | no | — | Disable form binding and only render layout. |
| children | `ReactElement` | yes | — | — |

**FormContextValue**

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| values | `FormValues` | yes | — | — |
| errors | `FormErrors` | yes | — | — |
| metas | `Record<string, FieldMeta \| undefined>` | yes | — | — |
| setFieldValue | `(name: string, value: unknown) => FormValues` | yes | — | Commits the change and returns the resulting values snapshot. |
| setFieldTouched | `(name: string, touched?: boolean) => void` | yes | — | — |
| validateField | `( name: string, nextValues?: FormValues, ) => Promise<string \| undefined>` | yes | — | Pass nextValues to validate a just-committed change. |
| registerField | `(name: string, rules: FormRule[]) => void` | yes | — | — |
| unregisterField | `(name: string) => void` | yes | — | — |
| getFieldValue | `(name: string) => unknown` | yes | — | — |
| validateOnChange | `boolean` | yes | — | — |
| formId | `string` | yes | — | — |

Type aliases:

- `FormRule` = `RuleObject`
- `FormErrors` = `Record<string, string | undefined>`
- `FormSubmitEvent` = `FormEvent<HTMLFormElement>`

#### Example: Validation

Keep field rules, values, and messages in one declarative form.

```tsx
import { Form, Button, Input } from "velune/react";

export function FormValidationExample() {
  const save = (values: Record<string, unknown>) => {
    console.log("Submitted values", values);
  };

  return (
    <>
      <Form initialValues={{ email: "" }} onSubmit={save}>
        <Form.Item
          name="email"
          rules={[{ required: true, message: "Email is required" }]}
        >
          <Input type="email" fullWidth>
            <Input.Label>Email</Input.Label>
          </Input>
        </Form.Item>
        <Button type="submit" fullWidth>Continue</Button>
      </Form>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Nested values

Bind compound value paths without manual state plumbing.

```tsx
import { Form, Input } from "velune/react";

export function FormNestedExample() {
  const save = (values: Record<string, unknown>) => {
    console.log("Submitted values", values);
  };

  return (
    <>
      <Form initialValues={{ profile: { name: "" } }} onSubmit={save}>
        <Form.Item name="profile.name">
          <Input fullWidth>
            <Input.Label>Display name</Input.Label>
          </Input>
        </Form.Item>
      </Form>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Standard Schema

Validate the whole values object with any Standard Schema library.

```tsx
import { Form, Button, Input } from "velune/react";

export function FormSchemaExample() {
  const emailSchema = {
    "~standard": {
      version: 1 as const,
      vendor: "example",
      validate(value: unknown) {
        const values = value as { email?: string };
        if (!values.email?.includes("@")) {
          return {
            issues: [{ message: "Enter a valid email", path: ["email"] }],
          };
        }
        return { value: values };
      },
    },
  };
  const save = (values: Record<string, unknown>) => {
    console.log("Submitted values", values);
  };

  return (
    <>
      <Form
        schema={emailSchema}
        initialValues={{ email: "" }}
        onSubmit={save}
      >
        <Form.Item name="email">
          <Input type="email" fullWidth>
            <Input.Label>Email</Input.Label>
            <Input.Description>Validated by a Standard Schema.</Input.Description>
          </Input>
        </Form.Item>
        <Button type="submit" fullWidth>Continue</Button>
      </Form>
    </>
  );
}
```

### Input

Capture text with sizes, clearable values, labels, adornments, and validation. Documentation: https://velune.dev/components/input

```tsx
import { Input } from "velune/react";
// or from the dedicated subpath:
import { Input } from "velune/react/input";
```

#### Props

**InputProps** (extends Omit< InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>, "children" | "size" | "prefix" >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |
| size | `InputSize` | no | md | Control height and type scale. Default: md. |
| clearable | `boolean` | no | — | Show a clear control when the field has a value. |
| clearLabel | `string` | no | Clear input | Accessible label for the clear control. Default: Clear input. |
| showPasswordLabel | `string` | no | Show password | Accessible label for revealing a password. Default: Show password. |
| hidePasswordLabel | `string` | no | Hide password | Accessible label for concealing a password. Default: Hide password. |
| invalid | `boolean` | no | — | Marks the field as invalid (aria-invalid). |
| fullWidth | `boolean` | no | — | Stretch the shell to 100% of the parent width. |

Type aliases:

- `InputSize` = `"sm" | "md" | "lg"`
- `InputLabelProps` = `LabelHTMLAttributes<HTMLLabelElement>`
- `InputPrefixProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>`
- `InputSuffixProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>`
- `InputDescriptionProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>`
- `InputErrorMessageProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>`

#### Example: Labels and description

Give every field a clear name and supporting context.

```tsx
import { Input } from "velune/react";

export function InputLabelsExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Input placeholder="you@company.com">
        <Input.Label>Email</Input.Label>
        <Input.Description>Used for account notifications.</Input.Description>
      </Input>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Affixes

Add contextual content without rebuilding the input shell.

```tsx
import { Input } from "velune/react";

export function InputAffixesExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Input defaultValue="velune">
        <Input.Label>Domain</Input.Label>
        <Input.Prefix>https://</Input.Prefix>
        <Input.Suffix>.com</Input.Suffix>
      </Input>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Validation states

Expose invalid and disabled states with accessible semantics.

```tsx
import { Input } from "velune/react";

export function InputStatesExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Input invalid>
        <Input.Label>Email</Input.Label>
        <Input.ErrorMessage>Enter a valid email</Input.ErrorMessage>
      </Input>
      <Input disabled defaultValue="Northstar">
        <Input.Label>Read-only workspace</Input.Label>
      </Input>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Clearable

Let users reset the field without selecting the text.

```tsx
import { Input } from "velune/react";

export function InputClearableExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Input defaultValue="Velune" clearable>
        <Input.Label>Workspace</Input.Label>
      </Input>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Sizes

Match field density to the surrounding layout.

```tsx
import { Input } from "velune/react";

export function InputSizesExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Input size="sm" placeholder="Small">
        <Input.Label>Small</Input.Label>
      </Input>
      <Input size="md" placeholder="Medium">
        <Input.Label>Medium</Input.Label>
      </Input>
      <Input size="lg" placeholder="Large">
        <Input.Label>Large</Input.Label>
      </Input>
    </>
  );
}
```

### Radio

Choose exactly one option from a related set. Documentation: https://velune.dev/components/radio

```tsx
import { Radio } from "velune/react";
// or from the dedicated subpath:
import { Radio } from "velune/react/radio";
```

#### Props

**RadioProps** (extends Omit< InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>, "type" | "value" | "size" >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| value | `RadioValue` | no | — | Value used inside Radio.Group. Required for group selection. |
| size | `RadioSize` | no | md | Visual size of the control + label. Default: md. |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |

**RadioGroupProps** (extends Omit< HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "children" | "defaultValue" | "dir" | "onChange" >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| name | `string` | no | — | — |
| form | `string` | no | — | Associates every radio with a form outside the group ancestry. |
| value | `RadioValue` | no | — | — |
| defaultValue | `RadioValue` | no | — | — |
| onValueChange | `(nextValue: RadioValue) => void` | no | — | — |
| size | `RadioSize` | no | size to every child radio | Applies a default size to every child radio. |
| orientation | `RadioGroupOrientation` | no | vertical | Layout of options. Default: vertical. |
| dir | `RadioGroupDirection` | no | ltr | Reading direction used by horizontal arrow navigation. Default: ltr. |
| loop | `boolean` | no | true | Wrap keyboard focus from the last option to the first. Default: true. |
| disabled | `boolean` | no | — | Disable every radio in the group. |
| required | `boolean` | no | — | Marks the radiogroup as required. |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |

Type aliases:

- `RadioValue` = `string`
- `RadioSize` = `"sm" | "md" | "lg"`
- `RadioGroupOrientation` = `"horizontal" | "vertical"`
- `RadioGroupDirection` = `"ltr" | "rtl"`
- `RadioDescriptionProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>`
- `RadioGroupLabelProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`
- `RadioGroupDescriptionProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLParagraphElement>`
- `RadioGroupErrorMessageProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLParagraphElement>`

#### Example: Radio group

Choose exactly one option from a related set.

```tsx
import { Radio } from "velune/react";

export function RadioGroupExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Radio.Group defaultValue="team" orientation="horizontal">
        <Radio value="personal">Personal</Radio>
        <Radio value="team">Team</Radio>
      </Radio.Group>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: States

Present selected and disabled options without custom behavior.

```tsx
import { Radio } from "velune/react";

export function RadioStatesExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Radio defaultChecked>Selected</Radio>
      <Radio disabled>Unavailable</Radio>
    </>
  );
}
```

### Select

Choose from searchable or grouped lists with Empty and NoMatches states. Documentation: https://velune.dev/components/select

```tsx
import { Select } from "velune/react";
// or from the dedicated subpath:
import { Select } from "velune/react/select";
```

#### Props

**SelectBaseProps** (extends Omit< HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "defaultValue" | "onChange" >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| children | `ReactNode` | yes | — | — |
| onSearch | `(query: string) => void` | no | — | Called when the search query changes. When provided, local filtering is skipped so you can drive remote search. |
| searchPlaceholder | `string` | no | Search… | Placeholder for the in-panel search field. Default: "Search…" |
| dir | `SelectDirection` | no | ltr | Reading direction for the control and portaled panel. Default: ltr. |
| size | `InputSize` | no | — | — |
| searchable | `boolean` | no | — | Enable search. Shows a sticky search field inside the dropdown. |
| portal | `boolean` | no | true | Render the options panel in a portal. Default: true. |
| invalid | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| disabled | `boolean \| undefined` | no | — | — |
| fullWidth | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| required | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| name | `string` | no | — | Native form field name. |
| form | `string` | no | — | Associates the control with a form outside its DOM ancestry. |
| autoComplete | `string` | no | — | — |
| aria-label | `string` | no | — | — |

**SelectSingleProps** (extends SelectBaseProps)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| multiple | `false` | no | — | — |
| value | `SelectValue` | no | — | — |
| defaultValue | `SelectValue` | no | — | — |
| onValueChange | `(value: SelectValue) => void` | no | — | — |

**SelectMultipleProps** (extends SelectBaseProps)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| multiple | `true` | yes | — | — |
| value | `SelectValue[]` | no | — | — |
| defaultValue | `SelectValue[]` | no | — | — |
| onValueChange | `(value: SelectValue[]) => void` | no | — | — |

**SelectTriggerProps** (extends ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| placeholder | `ReactNode` | no | — | Placeholder shown until a value is selected. |

**SelectItemProps** (extends HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| value | `SelectValue` | yes | — | — |
| disabled | `boolean \| undefined` | no | — | — |
| textValue | `string \| undefined` | no | — | Text used for typeahead and search when children are not plain text. |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |

**SelectGroupProps** (extends HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| textValue | `string \| undefined` | no | — | Text used for group-level search when the label is not plain text. |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |

Type aliases:

- `SelectValue` = `string`
- `SelectDirection` = `"ltr" | "rtl"`
- `SelectProps` = `SelectSingleProps | SelectMultipleProps`
- `SelectContentProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`
- `SelectGroupLabelProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`
- `SelectLabelProps` = `LabelHTMLAttributes<HTMLLabelElement>`
- `SelectDescriptionProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>`
- `SelectErrorMessageProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>`
- `SelectEmptyProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`
- `SelectNoMatchesProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`

#### Example: Options

Choose one value from a concise list.

```tsx
import { Select } from "velune/react";

export function SelectOptionsExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Select>
        <Select.Label>Role</Select.Label>
        <Select.Trigger placeholder="Choose a role" />
        <Select.Content>
          <Select.Item value="admin">Admin</Select.Item>
          <Select.Item value="editor">Editor</Select.Item>
        </Select.Content>
      </Select>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Searchable select

Filter larger option sets from the same keyboard-friendly control.

```tsx
import { Select } from "velune/react";

export function SelectSearchableExample() {
  const people = [
    { id: "ada", name: "Ada Lovelace" },
    { id: "grace", name: "Grace Hopper" },
    { id: "alan", name: "Alan Turing" },
  ];

  return (
    <>
      <Select searchable fullWidth>
        <Select.Label>Member</Select.Label>
        <Select.Trigger placeholder="Choose a member" />
        <Select.Content>
          {people.map((person) => (
            <Select.Item key={person.id} value={person.id}>
              {person.name}
            </Select.Item>
          ))}
        </Select.Content>
      </Select>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Empty options

Explain when the option list has nothing to choose from.

```tsx
import { Select } from "velune/react";

export function SelectEmptyExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Select>
        <Select.Label>Assignee</Select.Label>
        <Select.Trigger placeholder="Choose an assignee" />
        <Select.Empty>No assignees yet.</Select.Empty>
        <Select.Content />
      </Select>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: No search matches

Distinguish a fruitless filter from an empty option list.

```tsx
import { Select } from "velune/react";

export function SelectNoMatchesExample() {
  const people = [
    { id: "ada", name: "Ada Lovelace" },
    { id: "grace", name: "Grace Hopper" },
    { id: "alan", name: "Alan Turing" },
  ];

  return (
    <>
      <Select searchable fullWidth>
        <Select.Label>Member</Select.Label>
        <Select.Trigger placeholder="Choose a member" />
        <Select.NoMatches>No matching member.</Select.NoMatches>
        <Select.Content>
          {people.map((person) => (
            <Select.Item key={person.id} value={person.id}>
              {person.name}
            </Select.Item>
          ))}
        </Select.Content>
      </Select>
    </>
  );
}
```

### Switch

Toggle a setting immediately, with size, loading, and description support. Documentation: https://velune.dev/components/switch

```tsx
import { Switch } from "velune/react";
// or from the dedicated subpath:
import { Switch } from "velune/react/switch";
```

#### Props

**SwitchProps** (extends Omit< ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>, "onChange" | "value" | "type" >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| checked | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| defaultChecked | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| onCheckedChange | `(checked: boolean) => void` | no | — | — |
| value | `string` | no | on | Value submitted with name when checked. Default: "on". |
| required | `boolean` | no | — | Require the switch's submitted checkbox to be checked. |
| size | `SwitchSize` | no | — | — |
| loading | `boolean` | no | — | Locks interaction and shows a busy state on the thumb. |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |

Type aliases:

- `SwitchSize` = `"sm" | "md" | "lg"`
- `SwitchDescriptionProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>`

#### Example: Immediate setting

Apply a binary setting as soon as the control changes.

```tsx
import { useState } from "react";
import { Switch } from "velune/react";

export function SwitchControlledExample() {
  const [enabled, setEnabled] = useState(true);

  return (
    <>
      <Switch checked={enabled} onCheckedChange={setEnabled}>
        Notifications
      </Switch>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Sizes and states

Fit compact or large surfaces and communicate loading or unavailable settings.

```tsx
import { Switch } from "velune/react";

export function SwitchStatesExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Switch size="sm" defaultChecked>Compact</Switch>
      <Switch size="lg" defaultChecked>Large</Switch>
      <Switch loading>Saving preference</Switch>
      <Switch disabled>Unavailable</Switch>
      <Switch defaultChecked>
        Email digests
        <Switch.Description>
          Morning summary of workspace activity.
        </Switch.Description>
      </Switch>
    </>
  );
}
```

### Text Area

Capture longer text with resize modes, autosizing, and validation support. Documentation: https://velune.dev/components/text-area

```tsx
import { TextArea } from "velune/react";
// or from the dedicated subpath:
import { TextArea } from "velune/react/textarea";
```

#### Props

**TextAreaAutosize** (boolean | { minRows?: number; maxRows?: number; })

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| minRows | `number` | no | — | — |
| maxRows | `number` | no | — | — |

**TextAreaProps** (extends Omit< TextareaHTMLAttributes<HTMLTextAreaElement>, "children" | "size" >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |
| size | `InputSize` | no | md | Visual density. Default: md. |
| autosize | `TextAreaAutosize` | no | — | Grow with content. - true: browser field-sizing: content when supported - { minRows, maxRows }: JS-assisted clamp (with field-sizing fallback) |
| resize | `TextAreaResize` | no | vertical | Manual resize handle. Default: vertical. Ignored while autosize is enabled. |
| showCount | `boolean` | no | — | Show character count; pairs with maxLength when set. |
| invalid | `boolean` | no | — | Marks the field as invalid (aria-invalid). |
| fullWidth | `boolean` | no | — | Stretch to 100% of the parent width. |

Type aliases:

- `TextAreaResize` = `"none" | "vertical" | "horizontal" | "both"`
- `TextAreaLabelProps` = `LabelHTMLAttributes<HTMLLabelElement>`
- `TextAreaDescriptionProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>`
- `TextAreaErrorMessageProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>`

#### Example: Long-form input

Collect longer content with clear label and guidance.

```tsx
import { TextArea } from "velune/react";

export function TextAreaBasicExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <TextArea placeholder="What changed?">
        <TextArea.Label>Release notes</TextArea.Label>
      </TextArea>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Character count

Make length constraints visible while the user writes.

```tsx
import { TextArea } from "velune/react";

export function TextAreaCountExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <TextArea maxLength={160} showCount>
        <TextArea.Label>Summary</TextArea.Label>
      </TextArea>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Resize handle

Control whether users can resize the field manually.

```tsx
import { TextArea } from "velune/react";

export function TextAreaResizeExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <TextArea resize="both" defaultValue="Stretch in either direction.">
        <TextArea.Label>Freeform notes</TextArea.Label>
      </TextArea>
      <TextArea resize="none" defaultValue="Height stays fixed.">
        <TextArea.Label>Fixed height</TextArea.Label>
      </TextArea>
    </>
  );
}
```

## Navigation

### Breadcrumb

Show the current page location within a navigable hierarchy. Documentation: https://velune.dev/components/breadcrumb

```tsx
import { Breadcrumb } from "velune/react";
// or from the dedicated subpath:
import { Breadcrumb } from "velune/react/breadcrumb";
```

#### Props

**BreadcrumbProps** (extends HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| separator | `ReactNode` | no | — | Node rendered between items. Defaults to a chevron glyph. @example <Breadcrumb separator="/">…</Breadcrumb> |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |

**BreadcrumbItemProps** (extends Omit< LiHTMLAttributes<HTMLLIElement>, "onClick" >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| href | `string` | no | — | Renders the item content as a link. |
| target | `string` | no | — | — |
| current | `boolean` | no | — | Marks this item as the current page (aria-current="page"). When no item sets it explicitly, the last item is the current page. |
| disabled | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| onClick | `MouseEventHandler<HTMLElement>` | no | — | Fires on the item content (link or text). Ignored when disabled. |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |

#### Example: Page trail

The last item is treated as the current page automatically.

```tsx
import { Breadcrumb } from "velune/react";

export function BreadcrumbBasicExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Breadcrumb>
        <Breadcrumb.Item href="/">Home</Breadcrumb.Item>
        <Breadcrumb.Item href="/projects">Projects</Breadcrumb.Item>
        <Breadcrumb.Item>Northstar</Breadcrumb.Item>
      </Breadcrumb>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Custom separator

Swap the chevron for any node.

```tsx
import { Breadcrumb } from "velune/react";

export function BreadcrumbSeparatorExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Breadcrumb separator="/">
        <Breadcrumb.Item href="/">Home</Breadcrumb.Item>
        <Breadcrumb.Item href="/docs">Docs</Breadcrumb.Item>
        <Breadcrumb.Item>Components</Breadcrumb.Item>
      </Breadcrumb>
    </>
  );
}
```

### Pagination

Navigate large result sets, with optional hiding when only one page exists. Documentation: https://velune.dev/components/pagination

```tsx
import { Pagination } from "velune/react";
// or from the dedicated subpath:
import { Pagination } from "velune/react/pagination";
```

#### Props

**PaginationProps** (Omit<HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>, "onChange"> &)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| page | `number` | no | — | 1-based current page. |
| defaultPage | `number` | no | — | — |
| pageSize | `number` | no | — | — |
| defaultPageSize | `number` | no | — | — |
| total | `number` | yes | — | — |
| onPageChange | `(page: number, pageSize: number) => void` | no | — | — |
| simple | `boolean` | no | — | Compact prev/next only. |
| hideOnSinglePage | `boolean` | no | — | Render nothing when there is at most one page. |
| showSizeChanger | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| pageSizeOptions | `number[]` | no | — | — |
| showQuickJumper | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| siblingCount | `number` | no | 1 | Pages on each side of the current page. Default: 1. |
| disabled | `boolean` | no | — | — |
| aria-label | `string` | no | Pagination | Accessible name for the pagination landmark. Default: Pagination. |
| previousPageLabel | `string` | no | — | Accessible label for the previous-page command. |
| nextPageLabel | `string` | no | — | Accessible label for the next-page command. |
| getPageLabel | `(page: number) => string` | no | — | Builds the accessible label for a numbered page command. |
| rowsPerPageLabel | `ReactNode` | no | — | Visible label for the page-size selector. |
| goToPageLabel | `ReactNode` | no | — | Visible label before the quick-jump input. |
| jumpToPageLabel | `string` | no | — | Accessible label for the quick-jump input. |

#### Example: Pagination

Navigate a known result count with numbered pages.

```tsx
import { useState } from "react";
import { Pagination } from "velune/react";

export function PaginationDefaultExample() {
  const [page, setPage] = useState(4);

  return (
    <>
      <Pagination page={page} total={120} pageSize={10} onPageChange={setPage} />
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Simple pagination

Use compact previous and next controls when totals are secondary.

```tsx
import { useState } from "react";
import { Pagination } from "velune/react";

export function PaginationSimpleExample() {
  const [page, setPage] = useState(2);

  return (
    <>
      <Pagination page={page} total={50} pageSize={10} simple onPageChange={setPage} />
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Hide on a single page

Omit pagination when the result set fits on one page.

```tsx
import { useState } from "react";
import { Pagination } from "velune/react";

export function PaginationHideOnSinglePageExample() {
  const [page, setPage] = useState(1);

  return (
    <>
      <Pagination hideOnSinglePage total={8} pageSize={10} />
      <Pagination
        hideOnSinglePage
        page={page}
        total={80}
        pageSize={10}
        onPageChange={setPage}
      />
    </>
  );
}
```

### Sidebar

Composable application navigation rail with collapse, groups, menus, and mobile drawer behavior. Documentation: https://velune.dev/components/sidebar

```tsx
import { Sidebar } from "velune/react";
// or from the dedicated subpath:
import { Sidebar } from "velune/react/sidebar";
```

#### Props

**SidebarProviderProps** (extends HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| open | `boolean` | no | — | Controlled expanded state on desktop (true = expanded). |
| defaultOpen | `boolean` | no | true | Uncontrolled initial expanded state. Default: true. |
| onOpenChange | `(open: boolean) => void` | no | — | Called when the desktop expanded state changes. |
| enableKeyboardShortcut | `boolean` | no | — | When true (default), Ctrl/Cmd+B toggles the sidebar. Ignored while focus is in an editable field. |
| style | `CSSProperties` | no | — | Override CSS variables for sidebar widths. Supports --sidebar-width, --sidebar-width-icon, and --sidebar-width-mobile. |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |

**SidebarContextValue**

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| state | `SidebarState` | yes | — | Current desktop expand/collapse state. |
| open | `boolean` | yes | — | Whether the desktop sidebar is expanded. |
| setOpen | `(open: boolean \| ((value: boolean) => boolean)) => void` | yes | — | — |
| openMobile | `boolean` | yes | — | Whether the mobile drawer is open. |
| setOpenMobile | `(open: boolean \| ((value: boolean) => boolean)) => void` | yes | — | — |
| isMobile | `boolean` | yes | — | true below the md breakpoint. |
| toggleSidebar | `() => void` | yes | — | Toggles mobile drawer on small screens, desktop open state otherwise. |
| side | `SidebarSide` | yes | — | — |
| collapsible | `SidebarCollapsible` | yes | — | — |
| sidebarId | `string` | yes | — | DOM id wired to Sidebar.Trigger via aria-controls. |

**SidebarProps** (extends HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| side | `SidebarSide` | no | left | Which edge the sidebar anchors to. Default: left. |
| collapsible | `SidebarCollapsible` | no | offcanvas | Collapse behavior. - offcanvas: slides fully off-screen when collapsed - icon: collapses to an icon rail - none: never collapses Default: offcanvas. |
| aria-label | `string` | no | Sidebar | Accessible name for the navigation landmark. Default: "Sidebar". |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |

**SidebarGroupActionProps** (extends ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| as | `"button" \| "a"` | no | button | Render as a native button or anchor. Default: button. |
| href | `string` | no | — | — |

**SidebarMenuItemProps** (extends HTMLAttributes<HTMLLIElement>)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| open | `boolean` | no | — | Controlled open state when this item contains Sidebar.MenuSub. Items with a submenu are collapsible by default. |
| defaultOpen | `boolean` | no | false | Uncontrolled initial open state for collapsible submenu items. Default: false. |
| onOpenChange | `(open: boolean) => void` | no | — | Called when a collapsible submenu opens or closes. |

**SidebarMenuButtonProps** (extends Omit< ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>, "type" | "href" >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| as | `"button" \| "a"` | no | button | Render as a native button or anchor. Default: button. Ignored when the parent MenuItem contains MenuSub — submenu triggers are always buttons so they can toggle aria-expanded. |
| href | `string` | no | — | Required when as="a". |
| current | `boolean` | no | — | Marks the current page / selected destination. |
| tooltip | `ReactNode` | no | — | Tooltip shown when the sidebar is icon-collapsed. Prefer a string (also used as aria-label when collapsed) or pass aria-label explicitly for icon-only items. |
| type | `"button" \| "submit" \| "reset"` | no | — | — |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |

**SidebarMenuActionProps** (extends ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| as | `"button" \| "a"` | no | button | Render as a native button or anchor. Default: button. |
| href | `string` | no | — | — |
| showOnHover | `boolean` | no | false (always visible) | When true, the action is hidden until the menu item is hovered or focused. Default: false (always visible). |

**SidebarMenuSubButtonProps** (extends Omit< ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>, "type" | "href" >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| as | `"button" \| "a"` | no | button | Render as a native button or anchor. Default: button. |
| href | `string` | no | — | Required when as="a". |
| current | `boolean` | no | — | Marks the current nested destination. |
| type | `"button" \| "submit" \| "reset"` | no | — | — |

**SidebarTriggerProps** (extends ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| aria-label | `string` | no | depends on open state | Accessible name. Default depends on open state. |

Type aliases:

- `SidebarSide` = `"left" | "right"`
- `SidebarCollapsible` = `"offcanvas" | "icon" | "none"`
- `SidebarState` = `"expanded" | "collapsed"`
- `SidebarHeaderProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`
- `SidebarFooterProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`
- `SidebarContentProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`
- `SidebarGroupProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`
- `SidebarGroupLabelProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`
- `SidebarGroupContentProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`
- `SidebarMenuProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLUListElement>`
- `SidebarMenuBadgeProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`
- `SidebarMenuSubProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLUListElement>`
- `SidebarMenuSubItemProps` = `HTMLAttributes<HTMLLIElement>`

#### Example: Composition

Application shell with every public slot. Use `tooltip` for icon-collapsed labels, `Ctrl/Cmd+B` (opt out via `enableKeyboardShortcut={false}`) to toggle on desktop, and Drawer on mobile.

```tsx
import { Sidebar } from "velune/react";
import { Folder, Home, MoreHorizontal, Plus, User, Users } from "lucide-react";

export function SidebarCompositionExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <div className="min-h-svh">
        <Sidebar.Provider>
        <Sidebar collapsible="icon">
          <Sidebar.Header>
            <span className="flex size-gs-10 shrink-0 items-center justify-center rounded-gs-sm bg-gs-surface-mist text-gs-sm font-gs-medium">
              V
            </span>
          </Sidebar.Header>
          <Sidebar.Content>
            <Sidebar.Group>
              <Sidebar.GroupLabel>Workspace</Sidebar.GroupLabel>
              <Sidebar.GroupAction aria-label="Add project">
                <Plus size={16} />
              </Sidebar.GroupAction>
              <Sidebar.GroupContent>
                <Sidebar.Menu>
                  <Sidebar.MenuItem>
                    <Sidebar.MenuButton tooltip="Home">
                      <Home size={16} />
                      <span>Home</span>
                    </Sidebar.MenuButton>
                    <Sidebar.MenuAction aria-label="More" showOnHover>
                      <MoreHorizontal size={16} />
                    </Sidebar.MenuAction>
                  </Sidebar.MenuItem>
                  <Sidebar.MenuItem defaultOpen>
                    <Sidebar.MenuButton tooltip="Projects">
                      <Folder size={16} />
                      <span>Projects</span>
                    </Sidebar.MenuButton>
                    <Sidebar.MenuSub>
                      <Sidebar.MenuSubItem>
                        <Sidebar.MenuSubButton>Active</Sidebar.MenuSubButton>
                      </Sidebar.MenuSubItem>
                      <Sidebar.MenuSubItem>
                        <Sidebar.MenuSubButton>Archived</Sidebar.MenuSubButton>
                      </Sidebar.MenuSubItem>
                    </Sidebar.MenuSub>
                  </Sidebar.MenuItem>
                  <Sidebar.MenuItem>
                    <Sidebar.MenuButton current tooltip="Team">
                      <Users size={16} />
                      <span>Team</span>
                    </Sidebar.MenuButton>
                    <Sidebar.MenuBadge>8</Sidebar.MenuBadge>
                  </Sidebar.MenuItem>
                </Sidebar.Menu>
              </Sidebar.GroupContent>
            </Sidebar.Group>
          </Sidebar.Content>
          <Sidebar.Footer>
            <Sidebar.Menu>
              <Sidebar.MenuItem>
                <Sidebar.MenuButton tooltip="Account">
                  <User size={16} />
                  <span>Account</span>
                </Sidebar.MenuButton>
              </Sidebar.MenuItem>
            </Sidebar.Menu>
          </Sidebar.Footer>
        </Sidebar>
        <main className="relative flex min-h-full min-w-gs-0 flex-1 flex-col bg-gs-surface">
          <header className="flex h-gs-12 items-center gap-gs-2 border-b border-gs-border-default px-gs-4">
            <Sidebar.Trigger />
            <span className="text-gs-sm font-gs-medium">Overview</span>
          </header>
          <pre className="m-gs-0 overflow-auto p-gs-4 font-mono text-gs-xs leading-relaxed text-gs-text-secondary">{[
            "Sidebar.Provider",
            "├── Sidebar",
            "│   ├── Sidebar.Header",
            "│   ├── Sidebar.Content",
            "│   │   └── Sidebar.Group",
            "│       ├── Sidebar.GroupLabel",
            "│       ├── Sidebar.GroupAction",
            "│       └── Sidebar.GroupContent",
            "│           └── Sidebar.Menu",
            "│               └── Sidebar.MenuItem",
            "│                   ├── Sidebar.MenuButton",
            "│                   ├── Sidebar.MenuAction",
            "│                   ├── Sidebar.MenuBadge",
            "│                   └── Sidebar.MenuSub",
            "│                       └── Sidebar.MenuSubItem",
            "│                           └── Sidebar.MenuSubButton",
            "│   └── Sidebar.Footer",
            "└── (main)",
            "    └── Sidebar.Trigger",
          ].join("\n")}</pre>
        </main>
        </Sidebar.Provider>
      </div>
    </>
  );
}
```

### Tabs

Switch peer views using underline or block presentation. Documentation: https://velune.dev/components/tabs

```tsx
import { Tabs } from "velune/react";
// or from the dedicated subpath:
import { Tabs } from "velune/react/tabs";
```

#### Props

**TabsProps** (extends Omit< HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "onChange" >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| value | `TabsValue` | no | — | — |
| defaultValue | `TabsValue` | no | — | — |
| onValueChange | `(value: TabsValue) => void` | no | — | — |
| orientation | `TabsOrientation` | no | — | — |
| variant | `TabsVariant` | no | underline | Visual treatment. Default: underline. |
| activationMode | `"automatic" \| "manual"` | no | true | Activate tab on focus (arrow keys). Default: true. |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |

**TabsListProps** (extends HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| fullWidth | `boolean` | no | — | Stretch the header to its container and distribute tabs evenly. |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |

**TabsTriggerProps** (extends ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| value | `TabsValue` | yes | — | — |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |

**TabsPanelProps** (extends HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| value | `TabsValue` | yes | — | — |
| children | `ReactNode` | no | — | — |
| forceMount | `boolean` | no | false | Keep panel mounted when inactive. Default: false. |

Type aliases:

- `TabsOrientation` = `"horizontal" | "vertical"`
- `TabsVariant` = `"underline" | "block"`
- `TabsValue` = `string`

#### Example: Underline tabs

Switch peer views with a lightweight text treatment.

```tsx
import { Tabs } from "velune/react";

export function TabsUnderlineExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Tabs defaultValue="overview" variant="underline">
        <Tabs.List>
          <Tabs.Trigger value="overview">Overview</Tabs.Trigger>
          <Tabs.Trigger value="activity">Activity</Tabs.Trigger>
        </Tabs.List>
        <Tabs.Panel value="overview">Project overview</Tabs.Panel>
      </Tabs>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Block tabs

Use a filled segmented treatment for compact mode switching.

```tsx
import { Tabs } from "velune/react";

export function TabsBlockExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Tabs defaultValue="preview" variant="block">
        <Tabs.List fullWidth>
          <Tabs.Trigger value="preview">Preview</Tabs.Trigger>
          <Tabs.Trigger value="code">Code</Tabs.Trigger>
        </Tabs.List>
        <Tabs.Panel value="preview">Rendered result</Tabs.Panel>
        <Tabs.Panel value="code">Source code</Tabs.Panel>
      </Tabs>
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Example: Vertical tabs

Move navigation to the side for settings-style layouts.

```tsx
import { Tabs } from "velune/react";

export function TabsVerticalExample() {
  return (
    <>
      <Tabs defaultValue="general" orientation="vertical">
        <Tabs.List>
          <Tabs.Trigger value="general">General</Tabs.Trigger>
          <Tabs.Trigger value="security">Security</Tabs.Trigger>
        </Tabs.List>
        <Tabs.Panel value="general">Workspace preferences</Tabs.Panel>
        <Tabs.Panel value="security">Session settings</Tabs.Panel>
      </Tabs>
    </>
  );
}
```

### Wizard

Guide users through a sequenced multi-step workflow. Documentation: https://velune.dev/components/wizard

```tsx
import { Wizard } from "velune/react";
// or from the dedicated subpath:
import { Wizard } from "velune/react/wizard";
```

#### Props

**WizardStepMeta**

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| value | `WizardStepValue` | yes | — | — |
| title | `ReactNode` | yes | — | — |
| description | `ReactNode \| undefined` | no | — | — |
| optional | `boolean \| undefined` | no | — | — |
| disabled | `boolean \| undefined` | no | — | — |
| titleProps | `WizardTitleProps \| undefined` | no | — | — |
| descriptionProps | `WizardDescriptionProps \| undefined` | no | — | — |

**WizardNavigationContext**

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| from | `WizardStepValue` | yes | — | — |
| to | `WizardStepValue` | yes | — | — |
| fromIndex | `number` | yes | — | — |
| toIndex | `number` | yes | — | — |

**WizardProps** (extends Omit< HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "onChange" >)

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| currentStep | `WizardStepValue` | no | — | Controlled active step value. |
| defaultStep | `WizardStepValue` | no | — | Uncontrolled initial step. Defaults to the first step. |
| onStepChange | `(step: WizardStepValue, index: number) => void` | no | — | — |
| onBeforeNext | `( context: WizardNavigationContext, ) => boolean \| void \| Promise<boolean \| void>` | no | — | Called before advancing. Return false to cancel. Supports async validation (Promise). |
| onBeforePrev | `( context: WizardNavigationContext, ) => boolean \| void \| Promise<boolean \| void>` | no | — | Called before going back. Return false to cancel. |
